title: Cyber Security Plan Technical Control Attestation — MFA for Privileged Access

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
spl:
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 earliest=-24h
| lookup bes_privileged_users.csv Account_Name AS user OUTPUT requires_mfa bes_system
| where requires_mfa="true"
| eval strong_pkg=if(match(AuthenticationPackageName,"(?i)Negotiate|Kerberos") AND LogonType IN ("10","3"),1,0)
| stats count as logons sum(strong_pkg) as strong_signals by user, bes_system
| eval ratio=round(strong_signals/logons,3)
| where ratio < 0.9
value: We compare multifactor authentication signals for privileged BES accounts against the cyber security plan so documented controls match authentication reality.
implementation: (1) Enrich with IdP-specific fields if forwarded via HEC. (2) Tune `strong_pkg` logic with your MFA architecture. (3) Monthly report to the security working group. (4) Track remediation in ITSM.
visualization: Bar chart (ratio by user), Table (exceptions), Single value (users below threshold)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
